intent: DIVERGENCE FINDING - cross-version check reveals a real policy-content divergence. The rocky9-role-dyntransition scenario uses the context staff_u:system_r:newrole_t:s0, which is VALID in el9 (staff_u has roles: staff_r, sysadm_r, system_r, unconfined_r) but INVALID in el8 (staff_u has roles: staff_r, sysadm_r, unconfined_r - system_r is NOT in el8 staff_u). The el8 libsepol rejects the context with BADSCON (invalid security context) before it can classify the denial category. This is a real user-definition divergence between selinux-policy-3.14 (el8) and selinux-policy-38 (el9).
_kinds_note: authoritative_category for el8 is BADSCON (not a real bucket - the policy rejects the security context before replay). The el9 bucket is Constraint. This IS a divergence. The floor_label RoleSuspected is based on the record alone (role mismatch: staff_r vs system_r) and is correct for both versions.

oracle.audit2why_output:
libsepol.context_from_record: invalid security context: 'staff_u:system_r:newrole_t:s0'
libsepol.context_from_record: could not create context structure
libsepol.context_from_string: could not create context structure
libsepol.sepol_context_to_sid: could not convert staff_u:system_r:newrole_t:s0 to sid
oracle.el9_audit2why_detail: constrain process { transition noatsecure siginh rlimitinh dyntransition } ((r1 == r2 -Fail-) or ... (t1 == can_system_change -Fail-) and (r2 == system_r) ...) DENIED. Source role (staff_r) differs from target role (system_r).

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: Role constraint violation (el9) or invalid security context (el8). On el9, source role staff_r differs from target role system_r; the role constraint blocks it. On el8, the denial context is outright invalid (staff_u cannot use system_r in the el8 user definition). Either way, a TE allow cannot fix this.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: An impl that only looks at the AVC record (floor heuristic) sees staff_r vs system_r and correctly flags RoleSuspected on both versions. An authoritative-layer impl must handle both the Constraint case (el9) and the BADSCON case (el8) gracefully - BADSCON should be a distinct decline reason or at minimum treated as 'cannot classify, not a TE problem'.

xver_baseline.divergence_explanation: El8 selinux-policy-3.14 (el8_10) staff_u definition: roles {staff_r, sysadm_r, unconfined_r}. El9 selinux-policy-38 staff_u definition: roles {staff_r, sysadm_r, system_r, unconfined_r}. System_r was added to staff_u between selinux-policy-3.14 and 38. The tcontext staff_u:system_r:newrole_t:s0 is valid on el9 and can be replayed as a Constraint denial. On el8, libsepol rejects it as BADSCON (invalid security context) before any policy replay.
xver_baseline.policyvers_note: The policyvers difference (31 vs 33) is NOT the cause of the divergence. The cause is the selinux-policy USER DEFINITION of staff_u changing between RHEL 8 and RHEL 9. The AVC format and record itself is valid; the el8 policy simply does not recognize staff_u:system_r as a valid (user, role) combination.
xver_baseline.verified_by: seinfo --user staff_u -x on both el8 (fapolicyd8) and el9 (fapolicyd9) containers confirms the user definitions differ.
